whoAmI.title: Who I am
whoAmI.subtitle: A hybrid technical profile across sound, AV networking and infrastructure.
whoAmI.text1: I am Raúl López Penalva, a hybrid technical profile with experience in sound, audiovisual production, telecommunications, computing and technical infrastructure.
whoAmI.text2: I work at the intersection between the physical world of events and the logic of systems: sound, signal flow, networking, documentation, automation and operation. My approach is to understand each project as a complete system, not as a collection of isolated tasks.

origin.title: Where I come from
origin.text1: My relationship with technology started early, around computers, music, turntables, mixing desks and electronic equipment.
origin.text2: Music was the first catalyst. It led me to understand how a sound signal is captured, processed, amplified and delivered. From there, I started to look at technical systems not only as equipment, but as complete chains where every decision affects the final result.
origin.text3: That curiosity led me to study electricity, telecommunications, computing and sound for audiovisual production and live events. Over time, that training became real experience in events, film sets, technical installations and production environments.

technicalBackground.title: Technical background
technicalBackground.intro: My background combines several technical layers that now connect naturally in my work.
technicalBackground.trainingBullet1: Vocational training in Electrical Installations and Automation.
technicalBackground.trainingBullet2: Vocational training in Telecommunications and Computing Systems.
technicalBackground.trainingBullet3: Vocational training in Sound for Audiovisual Production and Live Events.
technicalBackground.trainingBullet4: Training in DevOps, CI/CD, infrastructure, containers and service operation.
technicalBackground.experienceBullet1: Work in real technical environments: events, film sets, installations, audiovisual production and internal infrastructure.
technicalBackground.experienceBullet2: Experience with live sound, signal routing, AV systems, technical maintenance and incident resolution.
technicalBackground.experienceBullet3: Collaboration with multidisciplinary teams under pressure, tight schedules and changing requirements.
technicalBackground.experienceBullet4: Ability to connect field operation, IP networking, documentation, automation and infrastructure.

bigPicture.title: The big picture as a common thread
bigPicture.text1: Since the beginning, I have had a clear obsession: understanding the complete system.
bigPicture.text2: In sound, that means looking beyond the mixing console. From the microphone to the audience. From the stage to the PA. From the patch to the network. From the technical decision to the final experience.
bigPicture.text3: The same applies to audiovisual production: a signal, a person, a machine or a poorly connected decision can affect the whole workflow.
bigPicture.bullet4: the critical points of the system
bigPicture.bottomText: Understanding that complete chain makes it possible to anticipate problems, coordinate better and make technical decisions with stronger criteria.

avToSystems.title: From audiovisual field work to systems
avToSystems.text1: For years I have worked on specific parts of complex systems: events, film sets, installations, technical maintenance, audiovisual production and internal infrastructure.
avToSystems.text2: Over time, I understood that my interest was not only in operating one part of the system, but in understanding how it is designed, how it connects, how it fails and how it can be improved.
avToSystems.text3: That curiosity naturally led me toward IP networking, AV systems, servers, Linux, Docker, automation, documentation and DevOps practices.
avToSystems.text4: I do not see it as moving to a different world, but as expanding the same technical criteria: designing better, deploying better, observing better and reducing improvisation.

learnedInRealSystems.title: What I learned from real technical environments
learnedInRealSystems.intro: Working in real environments teaches things that do not always appear in manuals or courses.
learnedInRealSystems.bullet1: Problems are rarely exactly where they appear.
learnedInRealSystems.bullet2: Optimizing one part of the system can make another worse if the whole is not understood.
learnedInRealSystems.bullet3: Pressure reveals whether a system has been well designed.
learnedInRealSystems.bullet4: Technical communication between people is as important as the tool.
learnedInRealSystems.bullet6: Improvisation is usually a symptom of poor planning.
learnedInRealSystems.bullet7: A good system must be operable, maintainable and explainable.
learnedInRealSystems.bullet8: Understanding the full flow saves more time than chasing the failure.

currentFocus.title: What I am focused on now
currentFocus.text1: My current professional focus is to consolidate a hybrid profile for events, audiovisual production and technical systems.
currentFocus.bullet1: Sound Engineering: mixing, operation and technical sound support.
currentFocus.bullet2: Sound System Engineering: verification, measurement and system optimization.
currentFocus.bullet3: AV Networking: IP networks for audio, video, control, streaming and production.
currentFocus.bullet4: Technical Planning: documentation, coordination and pre-production planning to reduce risk.
currentFocus.bottomText: Software, DevOps and infrastructure remain important, but not as a separate identity. I integrate them as another layer of my way of working: automating, documenting, deploying, monitoring and improving real technical systems.

howIWork.title: How I work
howIWork.text1: My way of working starts from a simple idea: before operating a system, you need to understand it.
howIWork.text2: I care about preparing before improvising, documenting before relying on memory, diagnosing before changing things randomly and designing solutions that can be maintained when real pressure appears.
howIWork.text3: In an event, an installation or a technical infrastructure, reliability does not appear by accident. It is built through criteria, order, communication and understanding of the complete system.
